title: Bucket Types, Endpoints, Regions, and S3 Express · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for bucket types, endpoints, regions, and s3 express and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bucket-type and endpoint compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.
Construct regional virtual-hosted and zonal endpoints without confusing region, availability zone, bucket name, TLS name, or path style.
Resolve intended region and endpoint explicitly and reject redirects, signing-region drift, and accidental cross-region transfer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go General Directory Bucket · changed case · Quiz General Directory Bucket
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 7632 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate general or directory bucket region, zone, endpoint, reactivation, and session rules; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.

Run the smallest deterministic S3, CloudFront, and Go storage decision model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the quiz General Directory Bucket case
Inject one changed S3, CloudFront, and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup and recovery, and state controlled AWS transfer limits for the quiz General Directory Bucket case

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Bucket Naming Creation Gate
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Bucket Naming Creation Gate: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 6818 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate general or directory bucket region, zone, endpoint, reactivation, and session rules; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate globally or zonally scoped naming, ownership, location, public-block, encryption, versioning, tags, and rollback before bucket creation.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Bucket Naming Creation Gate.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Bucket Naming Creation Gate, keep Bucket Naming Creation Gate connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Validate globally or zonally scoped naming, ownership, location, public-block, encryption, versioning, tags, and rollback before bucket creation.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Bucket Naming Creation Gate, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Bucket Naming Creation Gate precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Bucket Naming Creation Gate, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, custody, integrity, range, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 6818 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate general or directory bucket region, zone, endpoint, reactivation, and session rules; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate globally or zonally scoped naming, ownership, location, public-block, encryption, versioning, tags, and rollback before bucket creation.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Bucket Naming Creation Gate, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Creating a bucket is a harmless runtime operation that needs no reviewed infrastructure gate.
